target.title: feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
target.body:
## Summary

Surface **externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the running gateway** in `openclaw [gateway] status --deep`. Pure diagnostic, deep-only, read-only — no behavior change anywhere else.

## Motivation

`openclaw update` already syncs plugins via `updatePluginsAfterCoreUpdate`. But `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` bumps the gateway binary only — the postinstall script intentionally avoids touching plugin packages:

> `scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.mjs`: *"Plugin package dependencies are installed only by explicit plugin install/update flows, never postinstall."*

That's a deliberate design choice (postinstall is hot, plugin installs need network and can be slow). The unfortunate consequence: a gateway updated via `npm install -g openclaw@<v>` continues running silently mismatched against its externalized plugins until someone separately runs `openclaw plugins update <id>`.

In practice this just bit a real-world user (myself) on the **2026.5.3 → 2026.5.4** transition: gateway moved to 2026.5.4, `@openclaw/whatsapp` stayed on 2026.5.3 in `~/.openclaw-<profile>/npm/node_modules/` for ~6 minutes before I happened to notice it via `openclaw plugins list`. Nothing in `gateway status` or `channels status` flagged it; both reported healthy/connected. Easy class of latent bug.

This PR closes that gap by surfacing the drift in `status --deep` — the same surface that already reports gateway event-loop health, channel transport state, port issues, etc. Adding "plugin X is on 2026.5.3 but gateway is on 2026.5.4" fits the existing shape.

## Behavior

`openclaw gateway status --deep` (or `openclaw status --deep`) now includes a new section when drift is detected:

```
Plugin version drift: 1 externalized plugin not on gateway 2026.5.4
- whatsapp: 2026.5.3 (npm) → expected 2026.5.4
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.
```

When all plugins match, no extra output (silent). When run without `--deep`, no overhead at all. Bundled plugins (`origin: "bundled"`) ship with the gateway and are explicitly skipped — they cannot drift. Disabled plugins (`plugins.entries.<id>.enabled: false`) are also skipped since their version is not load-bearing.

## What this PR does **not** do

- ❌ No auto-update of plugins (deliberate; mirrors existing UX where channel/port issues are surfaced but not auto-resolved)
- ❌ No new CLI subcommand
- ❌ No change to the non-deep status path
- ❌ No new dependencies

## Files

- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts`** (new, 120 LOC): pure `detectPluginVersionDrift()` helper. Compares each enabled externalized (`npm` / `clawhub`) install record against the gateway `VERSION`. Build-suffix qualifiers (`-1`) are normalized identically on both sides so `2026.5.4-1 ≈ 2026.5.4`.
- **`src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts`** (new, 172 LOC): 10 unit tests covering:
  - match / drift / suffix normalization
  - ClawHub source coverage
  - ignored sources (archive/path/git — those are operator-pinned local artifacts)
  - missing-version skip (cannot fabricate a comparison)
  - disabled-in-config skip
  - default-enabled-by-absence
  - deterministic alphabetical sort
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts`**: populate a new optional `pluginVersionDrift` field on `DaemonStatus`, only when called with `deep: true`. Failure to read install records is non-fatal — status renders normally.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts`**: render the new section when drifts exist, with a fix hint pointing at the existing `openclaw plugins update` command.
- **`src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.ts`**: (no net change after rebase) the daemon-cli probe wrapper already surfaces the running gateway `server`/`version` from the handshake; drift now consumes it.

_No `CHANGELOG.md` change — release-note context lives in this PR body per the repo's release-owned changelog policy._

## Verification

- ✅ `tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.core.json` — no errors in any touched file
- ✅ Pre-commit hooks pass
- ✅ `vitest run` on `plugin-version-drift.test.ts`, `status.gather.test.ts`, `probe.test.ts` — **49/49 pass** (includes 4 new gather regression tests)
- ✅ Rebased clean onto current `main`

## Review response (revision after ClawSweeper review)

Addressed all three findings:

- **[P2] Compare drift against the running Gateway version** — drift now uses the running gateway version resolved from the probe handshake (`rpc.server?.version` / `rpc.version`), falling back to the invoking CLI `VERSION` only when no gateway version is available. This removes the false-positive where a newer CLI probing an older still-running gateway flagged plugins that actually match the gateway.
- **[P2] Read install records from the daemon service environment** — `loadInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecords({ env: mergedDaemonEnv })` now reads the managed service's profile/state dir instead of the invoking CLI process's, so a CLI running under a different profile no longer produces missed/false drift.
- **[P3] Remove the CHANGELOG.md edit** — done; the branch no longer touches `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **Dirty branch** — rebased onto current `main`; conflicts resolved (the obsolete schema-snapshot workaround commit was dropped as it's no longer needed against current main).
- Added focused `status.gather` regression tests for the running-version and profile-mismatch cases.

## Why I think this belongs in core (not as a ClawHub skill)

This is filling a gap in an existing core diagnostic surface, not introducing new behavior. The same `status --deep` already reports gateway event-loop degraded states, channel transport issues, port conflicts, and stale gateway PIDs — adding plugin version drift fits that exact pattern. A ClawHub skill could implement the same detection externally, but the natural home for "is everything OK with my gateway?" is `openclaw status --deep`, where users already look.

Happy to adjust scope, naming, or output format based on review.



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Externalized npm/ClawHub plugins whose installed version doesn't match the **running gateway** weren't surfaced anywhere. After review, drift is computed against the running gateway version (from the probe handshake), not the invoking CLI `VERSION`, and install records are read from the managed daemon service's profile/state dir. The detector still skips version-matched, explicitly-disabled, and non-externalized (path/bundled) plugins.

**Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin arm64, Mac Studio), Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Branch `feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep` rebased onto current `main` (head `e6074c9`), `pnpm install` completed. The real `detectPluginVersionDrift` module from `src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.ts` was imported and executed via `tsx` (no mocks), driven by a realistic install index. The CLI/Gateway version split and the service-env install-record read are additionally covered by real `status.gather` tests that drive `gatherDaemonStatus` with a mocked probe returning `server.version` and a daemon service env distinct from the CLI process env.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
$ git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
feat/plugin-version-drift-status-deep
$ git log --oneline -2
e6074c9 fix(status): compute plugin drift from running gateway + service env
3358da8 feat(status): detect externalized plugin version drift in --deep
$ npx tsx proof-harness.mts                          # real detection module, bug-vs-fix
$ npx vitest run src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts \
                 src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.test.ts \
                 src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts
```

Scenario: invoking CLI is **2026.5.6**, the still-running gateway is **2026.5.4**. Install index: `@openclaw/whatsapp`=2026.5.3 (npm, behind running gateway), `@openclaw/discord`=2026.5.4 (npm, matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal`=2026.5.2 (npm, but disabled in config), `context-topics` source `path`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live stdout copied from the run against the real module:

```
=== OLD behavior (gatewayVersion = CLI VERSION) — the bug ===
Against CLI 2026.5.6, discord(2026.5.4) is WRONGLY flagged as drift: [ '@openclaw/discord', '@openclaw/whatsapp' ]

=== NEW behavior (gatewayVersion = running gateway from probe) ===
{
  "gatewayVersion": "2026.5.4",
  "drifts": [
    {
      "pluginId": "@openclaw/whatsapp",
      "installedVersion": "2026.5.3",
      "gatewayVersion": "2026.5.4",
      "source": "npm",
      "packageName": "@openclaw/whatsapp"
    }
  ]
}

=== Rendered `openclaw status --deep` drift section (fixed) ===
Plugin version drift: 1 externalized plugin not on gateway 2026.5.4
- @openclaw/whatsapp: 2026.5.3 (npm) → expected 2026.5.4
Fix: openclaw plugins update <plugin-id> for each drifted plugin, then openclaw gateway restart.

=== Assertions ===
running-gateway version used:                   true
whatsapp reported (behind running gateway):     true
discord NOT reported (matches running gateway): true
signal NOT reported (disabled):                 true
context-topics NOT reported (path):             true
no false positive vs CLI version:               true

(test suite)
 ✓ src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts (10 tests)
 ✓ src/cli/daemon-cli/probe.test.ts (11 tests)
 ✓ src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts (28 tests)
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  49 passed (49)
```

**Observed result after fix:** Under the old logic (CLI `VERSION` = 2026.5.6) `@openclaw/discord` was **wrongly** flagged as drifted even though it matches the running gateway. After the fix (running gateway version = 2026.5.4), only the genuinely-behind `@openclaw/whatsapp` is reported; `@openclaw/discord` (matches running gateway), `@openclaw/signal` (disabled), and `context-topics` (non-externalized `path`) are all correctly omitted — no false positives. The `status --deep` drift section renders verbatim in the expected format. The full suite (drift + probe + gather, including the 4 new regression tests exercising the running-version and service-env paths) passes 49/49.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end invocation of the live `openclaw gateway status --deep` CLI binary against a physically version-mismatched on-disk plugin install under a separate running gateway service. The harness drives the real detection module directly, and the gather-level wiring (running gateway version from the probe + service-env install-record read) is covered by real `status.gather` tests rather than a live two-process CLI run.

target.title: fix: remove isOpenAIProvider gate from applyPatchEnabled
target.body:
## Bug

apply_patch was unconditionally gated to only work with OpenAI providers (openai, openai-codex) via isOpenAIProvider(). This prevented the tool from being available on kimi, anthropic, qwen, stepfun, and all other providers.

## Root cause

In src/agents/agent-tools.ts, the applyPatchEnabled logic included a check that only allowed the tool for OpenAI providers.

## Fix

Remove the isOpenAIProvider check. The apply_patch format is client-side (*** Begin Patch / *** End Patch markers) and is not model-specific. The remaining isApplyPatchAllowedForModel check still allows per-model allowlisting via tools.exec.applyPatch.allowModels.

## Changes

- Removed isOpenAIProvider function definition (no longer needed)
- Removed isOpenAIProvider(options?.modelProvider) from applyPatchEnabled
- Updated test expectations in agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts to match new behavior

## Fixes

- Fixes #88357
- Closes #45269 (related — same underlying issue of apply_patch being treated as provider-specific)

## Verification

- Tool is now created for all providers when enabled
- isApplyPatchAllowedForModel still filters by allowModels if configured
- No functional change for OpenAI providers
- Tests updated and passing
- Branch merged with latest main, conflicts resolved

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** apply_patch tool was gated to only work with OpenAI providers (openai, openai-codex) via isOpenAIProvider(). This prevented non-OpenAI providers like kimi, anthropic, qwen, and stepfun from using the apply_patch tool even when explicitly enabled in config.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw gateway 2026.5.27 (27ae826) running on Linux (Ubuntu 24.04), agent:engineer session, kimi/kimi-for-coding provider (non-OpenAI).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Started a new session with kimi/kimi-for-coding provider
2. Created a test file: echo "hello world" > test-apply-patch.txt
3. Invoked the edit tool (which uses apply_patch internally) to modify the file
4. Verified the file was modified correctly

**Evidence after fix:**
```
🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.27 (27ae826)
🧠 Model: kimi/kimi-for-coding · 🔑 api-key (kimi:default)
⚙️ Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenClaw Pi Default

> edit test-apply-patch.txt
> Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in test-apply-patch.txt

$ cat test-apply-patch.txt
hello world
this is a test of apply_patch on kimi provider
```

**Observed result after fix:** The edit tool (which internally uses apply_patch) successfully modified the file on the kimi provider. The tool call completed without errors and the file content was updated as expected. This confirms apply_patch is now available and functional on non-OpenAI providers.

**What was not tested:** Direct apply_patch tool invocation via the raw patch format (*** Begin Patch / *** End Patch markers) — only tested through the edit tool wrapper. Other non-OpenAI providers (anthropic, qwen, stepfun, google) not explicitly tested, but the code change removes the provider gate universally.

target.title: fix(hooks): pass media metadata to internal message_received hook
target.body:
## Problem

Commit 65e2120f8c (PR #87297) added six missing media metadata fields
(`mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaType`, `mediaPaths`, `mediaUrls`,
`mediaTypes`) to the **plugin** `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent` mapper,
but the parallel **internal** `toInternalMessageReceivedContext` mapper
was not updated.

Internal hooks registered via `registerInternalHook("message:received",
...)` receive a `MessageReceivedHookContext` whose `metadata` object is
built by `toInternalMessageReceivedContext`. Because the media fields
were omitted, internal hook consumers lose all media metadata on
received messages, even though the canonical context carries the data.

The plugin path and the inbound-claim path both surface these fields
correctly; only the internal hooks path is affected.

## Fix

Add the same six media fields to the `metadata` object in
`toInternalMessageReceivedContext`, matching the plugin mapper that was
fixed in 65e2120f8c.

Production diff is +6 lines in `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.ts`, +8
lines of test assertions in the corresponding test file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Internal hooks `message:received` context now
includes all six media metadata fields (`mediaPath`, `mediaUrl`,
`mediaType`, `mediaPaths`, `mediaUrls`, `mediaTypes`) in its metadata
object, matching the plugin path fixed in PR #87297.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built
from source (commit 01ef169004 + this patch) at `/tmp/fix-internal-hooks-media`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1: Install deps and run targeted tests
```console
$ cd /tmp/fix-internal-hooks-media
$ CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts
```

Step 2: Type-check the core project
```console
$ pnpm tsgo:core
```

Step 3: Build OpenClaw from patched source
```console
$ pnpm build
```

Step 4: Verify the fix is in compiled production code
```console
$ grep -n 'toInternalMessageReceivedContext' dist/message-hook-mappers-*.js
$ sed -n '229,258p' dist/message-hook-mappers-*.js
```

Step 5: Start the patched gateway to confirm module loads cleanly
```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Vitest results (13/13 pass, including new media assertions):**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.hooks.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/fix-internal-hooks-media

 ✓ |hooks| src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts (13 tests) 43ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
   Start at  11:29:28
   Duration  828ms (transform 564ms, setup 192ms, import 519ms, tests 43ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.52s
```

**Type check passes:**

```console
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```

**Compiled production code shows all six media fields in `toInternalMessageReceivedContext`:**

```console
$ sed -n '229,258p' dist/message-hook-mappers-D_6ymTKa.js
function toInternalMessageReceivedContext(canonical) {
return {
from: canonical.from,
content: canonical.content,
timestamp: canonical.timestamp,
channelId: canonical.channelId,
accountId: canonical.accountId,
conversationId: canonical.conversationId,
messageId: canonical.messageId,
metadata: {
to: canonical.to,
provider: canonical.provider,
surface: canonical.surface,
threadId: canonical.threadId,
senderId: canonical.senderId,
senderName: canonical.senderName,
senderUsername: canonical.senderUsername,
senderE164: canonical.senderE164,
mediaPath: canonical.mediaPath,
mediaUrl: canonical.mediaUrl,
mediaType: canonical.mediaType,
mediaPaths: canonical.mediaPaths,
mediaUrls: canonical.mediaUrls,
mediaTypes: canonical.mediaTypes,
guildId: canonical.guildId,
channelName: canonical.channelName,
topicName: canonical.topicName
}
};
}
```

**Gateway starts and shuts down cleanly with patched module:**

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-31T11:31:41.964-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-31T11:31:41.995-07:00 [gateway] resolving authentication…
2026-05-31T11:31:42.002-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-31T11:31:42.539-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-05-31T11:31:42.565-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-31T11:31:51.464-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-31T11:31:51.464-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-31T11:31:51.465-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-31T11:31:51.465-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM during shutdown; ignoring
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled `toInternalMessageReceivedContext`
function now includes all six media metadata fields (`mediaPath`,
`mediaUrl`, `mediaType`, `mediaPaths`, `mediaUrls`, `mediaTypes`) in its
`metadata` return object, matching `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent`. The
gateway loads the patched module cleanly. Tests confirm the fields
propagate through with correct values for both single-attachment and
multi-attachment scenarios.

What was not tested: Live message delivery through a channel provider
(WhatsApp, Telegram) with actual media attachments, because that requires
a configured provider account. The fix is a pure data-forwarding change
with no conditional logic.

target.title: docs(sms): add Twilio A2P delivery guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a short US A2P / 10DLC delivery section to the SMS channel docs.
- Clarify that OpenClaw SMS setup and US carrier/Twilio A2P approval are separate readiness gates.
- Add troubleshooting guidance for Twilio sends that are accepted by the API but later show `undelivered`, including `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** SMS users can complete OpenClaw webhook/pairing/outbound setup while US carrier delivery still fails because Twilio A2P 10DLC Brand, Campaign, Messaging Service, or sender-number registration is incomplete. This docs change makes that separate delivery gate visible in the SMS channel docs and points users to Twilio's source-of-truth registration/error docs.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `clawsean/sms-a2p-docs`, rebased onto upstream `origin/main` at `7e0d275f7add659d62515c382c112fcf7d84a599`. The edited page is `docs/channels/sms.md`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD -- docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/channels/sms.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real local checkout after the docs patch:

```text
Docs MDX check passed (1 files, 136ms).
Docs formatting clean (660 files).
checked_internal_links=4544
broken_links=0
```

Also manually reviewed the current Twilio A2P 10DLC overview, registration quickstart, required business/campaign information, and Twilio error pages for `30034`, `30909`, and `30923`. Claude Foreman also reviewed the wording for coverage, flow, and legal-advice risk; verdict was "ready with small edits", and those edits were applied before opening this PR.

**Observed result after fix:** The SMS docs page parses as MDX, passes docs formatting, has no broken internal links, and now contains a concise A2P/10DLC prerequisite section plus a targeted troubleshooting note for Twilio API acceptance followed by carrier delivery failure.

**What was not tested:** I did not send live SMS for this docs-only change. I did not test a Twilio A2P campaign approval flow in the Twilio Console. I did not run full repo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR only changes one docs page and the targeted docs checks above passed.

## AI-assisted

This PR is AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated wording, checked it against Twilio/OpenClaw docs, and applied a second-pass Claude Foreman review before opening.

target.title: fix(feishu): fallback when accepted turns send no visible reply
target.body:
## Summary

- add Feishu dispatcher-visible reply disposition tracking for accepted turns
- send an explicit fallback when a dispatched Feishu turn completes without any visible output
- suppress that fallback for intentional silent/NO_REPLY finals and for already-visible streaming/card replies
- add targeted regression tests for empty completion, intentional silence, visible streaming close, and per-reply state reset

## Why

Feishu/Lark accepted inbound turns can currently end with no user-visible output when the runtime aborts, a restart interrupts before visible content, or streaming-card terminalization fails before content is shown. In those cases the user sees silence even though the message was accepted and dispatched.

This patch makes Feishu track whether the dispatcher actually delivered visible output, rather than treating `counts.final === 0` alone as proof of silence. The bot still uses the zero-final dispatch completion as the trigger point, but the dispatcher guard prevents false fallbacks after visible streaming/card output or intentional NO_REPLY.

## Tests

Lightweight local checks on a constrained VPS:

- ✅ TypeScript transpile smoke for changed files:
  - `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts`
- ✅ `git diff --check`
- ⚠️ Targeted vitest was attempted with:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts --runInBand`
  - It did not complete on the VPS: workspace dependency hydration retried several native tarballs and the process was SIGKILLed. I did not treat that as a test pass; CI should validate the targeted test file.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Feishu accepted inbound turns could complete without user-visible output after interruption / restart / streaming-card empty terminalization. The fix ensures an explicit visible fallback is sent when no visible reply was delivered, while preserving intentional NO_REPLY/silent behavior.

- **Real environment tested**: Live OpenClaw gateway on VPS host `iv-yegrn5k9vky6d72c8f3t`, Feishu account `default`, real Feishu group chat runtime. Local runtime hotfix was applied to the installed OpenClaw/Lark runtime and the gateway was restarted.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Applied the local runtime hotfix equivalent to the PR behavior, restarted the live OpenClaw gateway, then observed real Feishu runtime logs for an accepted turn reaching the empty-reply streaming fallback path instead of staying silent.

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime log excerpt from the live setup:

```text
2026-05-28T19:26:20+08:00 gateway tool: restart requested (reason=Load narrowed Feishu no-reply fallback hotfix: skip fallback for intentional silent final replies via onSkip reason tracking.)
2026-05-28T19:32:05+08:00 feishu[default][msg:om_x100b6eb62b75acacc4a0c55586a37f3]: reply completed without visible text, using empty-reply fallback
```

Local evidence artifacts retained on the live host:

```text
/root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/task-runtime/T-20260528-RUNTIME-REPLY-INTEGRITY-HOTFIX/validation-after-restart.md
/root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/task-runtime/T-20260528-RUNTIME-REPLY-INTEGRITY-HOTFIX/wrapup-2026-05-29.md
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The real Feishu runtime no longer left that empty terminalization path silent; it reached the visible empty-reply fallback path after the hotfix/restart. The upstream PR turns that local behavior into dispatcher-level visible-output tracking so duplicate fallback is suppressed after already-visible card/text output and intentional silent finals are not converted into user-visible warnings.

- **What was not tested**: The exact upstream TypeScript patch was not package-released and installed into the live gateway yet; targeted unit coverage is included in this PR and CI is expected to validate it.

target.title: [codex] Read exact X posts via FxTwitter
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a key-free FxTwitter exact-post reader for public X/Twitter status URLs and numeric status IDs.
- Route exact post references through FxTwitter before falling back to xAI-backed broader `x_search`.
- Keep generic X search on the existing xAI auth path and update docs/tests/build guards for the new behavior.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw can read an exact public X/Twitter status URL through `x_search` without requiring an xAI API key for that exact post read.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw Gateway/Dashboard, built from this patch, restarted under LaunchAgent, listening on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the promoted runtime snapshot.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built OpenClaw, restarted the Gateway, verified `openclaw gateway status --deep`, then invoked the live Gateway HTTP tool endpoint with `curl` against `x_search` using `https://x.com/jack/status/20`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real Gateway invocation:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "provider": "fxtwitter",
  "mode": "exact_post",
  "statusId": "20",
  "hasExpectedText": true
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Gateway returned `provider=fxtwitter`, `mode=exact_post`, `statusId=20`, and confirmed the expected post text was present (`just setting up my twttr`). This proves the exact-post path used FxTwitter and worked on a real running OpenClaw setup.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the exact public post-read path. Broader xAI-backed generic X search remains covered by targeted tests and still requires xAI auth.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test src/infra/tsdown-config.test.ts test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/startup-context.test.ts extensions/xai/x-search.test.ts extensions/xai/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm build`

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body:
Fixes #69737.

## Summary

This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary.

It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to:

```text
LLM request failed.
```

Terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use the safe wrapper for actual assistant error turns. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers.

The follow-up repair preserves aborted-without-error behavior by keeping non-error aborted turns on the existing formatter path, so an aborted assistant with no `errorMessage` does not create a synthetic generic error payload.

## Evidence

- Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737`
- Current HEAD tested: `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`
- Raw provider-error suppression remains scoped to `stopReason="error"`.
- Aborted-without-error payload behavior is preserved.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text. The repair also preserves aborted-without-error behavior so no synthetic generic error payload is emitted for aborted assistant turns with no `errorMessage`.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f`, running the actual `handleAgentEnd` lifecycle function and actual `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` reply payload builder through `node --import tsx`, plus a live Telegram DM through my existing OpenClaw Telegram bot.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Direct Node/tsx proof imported `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts`, passed a terminal assistant payload with `stopReason: "error"`, `content: []`, and `errorMessage: "SECRET_CANARY_69737"`, then captured user-facing lifecycle event text and reply payload text. For Telegram, the PR checkout was started locally, `openai/gpt-5.5` was temporarily routed to a local OpenAI Responses-compatible endpoint at `127.0.0.1:8787/v1`, and a live Telegram DM triggered a provider 500 containing `SECRET_CANARY_69737`.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the direct proof command and live Telegram gateway log excerpt:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx direct handleAgentEnd plus buildEmbeddedRunPayloads canary proof",
  "head": "5bb04e6997717541b9c0ede4f0abf7784f49f06f",
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingLifecycleText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingLifecycleText": true,
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": true,
  "replyPayloads": [
    {
      "text": "LLM request failed.",
      "isError": true
    }
  ],
  "internalRawPreviewRetainedForMaintainers": true
}
```

```text
[openai-transport] [responses] error provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=500 code=telegram_live_proof_canary type=server_error message=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737
[telegram] embedded run agent end: isError=true model=gpt-5.5 provider=openai error=LLM request failed. rawError=500 SECRET_CANARY_69737
```

![Live Telegram proof screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f223a2e-76f5-430f-b99c-1be7f2045063)

- Observed result after fix: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` is absent from both user-facing lifecycle text and user-facing reply payload text. The live Telegram transcript displays a generic failure response and does not show `SECRET_CANARY_69737`. Internal diagnostic preview retention still reports true for maintainers. The aborted-without-error regression is covered by the added payload regression test and preserves the no-payload behavior.

- What was not tested: Slack delivery was not exercised. The direct lifecycle/payload behavior and live Telegram DM delivery path were exercised for this PR scope.

## Testing

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  156 passed (156)
```

```bash
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

commands.author: wAngByg

target.title: feat(trajectory): allow OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override for per-event byte cap
target.body:
## Summary

The per-event byte cap for trajectory recording (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES`) is
hard-coded to 256 KiB in two independent locations:

- `src/trajectory/paths.ts:8` — consumed by the core runtime writer
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts:35` — independent copy in the Codex app-server writer

This makes it impossible to raise the limit without rebuilding from source.
Heavy sessions (large `context.compiled` payloads, multi-tool responses) routinely
exceed 256 KiB and lose diagnostic data.

This PR:

1. Reads `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES` at startup in both writers,
   falling back to the current 256 KiB default.
2. Both core and Codex resolvers accept human-friendly suffixes (`512kb`, `2mb`, `1gb`)
or raw byte counts (`1048576`).
3. Adds unit tests in the Codex extension confirming the override and suffix paths.
4. Documents the new variable in `docs/tools/trajectory.md`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** trajectory events silently truncated at 256 KiB with no
user-configurable override.

**Environment tested:** Linux 6.8.0-106-generic (x64), OpenClaw v2026.5.7 (eeef486),
Node v24.14.1, 1212 trajectory files over 30 days.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l

node -e "const fs=require('fs');const d=fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');const lines=d.split('
').filter(Boolean);const t=lines.filter(l=>{const o=JSON.parse(l);return o.data?.truncated});console.log('total:',lines.length,'truncated:',t.length)" ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/9e600bba-2bbe-4ff4-98ca-59ea5f1bb896.trajectory.jsonl
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before — same production host as §"Environment tested", current published `openclaw` build, last 30 days of sessions:

```text
$ grep -c '"truncated":true' ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.trajectory.jsonl | grep -v ':0$' | wc -l
459
```

A single heavy session (7.3 MB transcript) had 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss under the default 256 KiB cap.

Earlier after-fix driver — patched branch head `76877f65f9c42b1154820dba43838ea413b3dc27`, a self-contained driver that wires the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and records a synthetic oversized payload (60 keys × 5000 chars = ~293 KB after sanitization, deliberately under the inner `STRING_MAX_CHARS=32768` / `OBJECT_MAX_KEYS=64` / `DEPTH=6` limits so only the outer event-cap fires). The two phases use the **identical payload**; only the env var changes:

```text
==================================================
PHASE — default 256 KiB cap, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935876980.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=(unset)
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 354
>>> truncated flag: true
>>> truncation originalBytes: 301095
>>> truncation limitBytes:    262144
>>> truncation reason:        trajectory-event-size-limit

==================================================
PHASE — OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb, identical payload
==================================================
>>> recorder.filePath = /root/openclaw-80681/pr80681-driver-1778935877004.trajectory.jsonl
>>> resolved env override: OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb
>>> payload: 60 keys × 5000 chars = 300000 chars total (~293 KB)
>>> recorded line bytes: 301095
>>> truncated flag: false
>>> payload key count preserved: 60
```

Current head validation — patched source checkout at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045`, Windows NT 10.0.26100 x64, Node v24.15.0, using the real `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` path and the same oversized payload shape:

```text
PHASE default-cap
env (unset)
bytes 312
truncated true
reason trajectory-event-size-limit
field_count (truncated sentinel)

PHASE one-mb
env 1mb
bytes 301033
truncated false
reason (none)
field_count 60
```

Current head focused tests also pass after the Windows-portable test expectation fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/paths.test.ts src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed | 2 skipped (13)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed (9)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 19.80s
```

- Phase 1 (no override): the recorded `.trajectory.jsonl` line is replaced by a 354-byte sentinel carrying `truncated:true`, `originalBytes:301095`, `limitBytes:262144`, `reason:"trajectory-event-size-limit"`. This is the current `main` behavior under the default 256 KiB cap.
- Phase 2 (`OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1mb`): same payload, same recorder. The recorded line is the full **301095 bytes** and carries `truncated:false`; all 60 fields survive at full 5000 chars each.

The byte-for-byte difference (354 → 301095, ~880×) is the direct manifestation of the override raising the per-event cap above the actual payload size. The current core-runtime fix at PR head `f6ae71e323f5523e9ecdb894d6990d0ca5639045` resolves the cap once from `params.env` at recorder creation (ClawSweeper P2), so the override is honored across the recorder lifetime rather than being re-read from ambient `process.env` per event.

The earlier focused vitest set also passed on the previous patch build:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.6

 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the 256 KiB default when env is empty  125ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > returns the default when the override is whitespace only  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts raw byte counts  1ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts kb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts mb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > accepts gb suffixes  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on unparseable input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > falls back to the default on non-positive input  0ms
 ✓ unit src/trajectory/paths.test.ts > resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes > module-load constant matches the empty-env resolver result  0ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > keeps write flags usable when O_NOFOLLOW is unavailable  9ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > records by default unless explicitly disabled  5ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > sanitizes session ids when resolving an override directory  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors explicit disablement  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > refuses to append through a symlinked parent directory  1ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override  3ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > truncates events that exceed the runtime event byte limit  4ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > accepts human-friendly byte-size suffixes for the event cap override  2ms
 ✓ extensions extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts > Codex trajectory recorder > falls back to default on invalid suffix values  2ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  16.93s
```

The new test case `honors OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES override` directly exercises the env-injection path at recorder creation.

**Observed result:** 459 out of 1212 trajectory files contain truncated events
(reason: `trajectory-event-size-limit`). A single heavy session
(`9e600bba`) has 633 truncated events out of 1891 total — 33% data loss.
After the patch, setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=1048576`
eliminates truncation for sessions that fit within 1 MiB per event.
Setting `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES=2mb` is equivalent.

**Not tested:** Filesystem-level I/O performance impact at very large per-event limits
(>10 MiB). The file-level cap (`TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_FILE_MAX_BYTES = 50 MiB`) still
applies and prevents unbounded disk growth.
## Addressing ClawSweeper review

### P2 — Resolve the core cap from the recorder env (`src/trajectory/runtime.ts:121`)

Fixed. `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` already captured `params.env` for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` enablement and trajectory path resolution; the truncation path was re-reading the per-event byte cap from ambient `process.env` on every event, which (a) silently ignored an injected `params.env` override and (b) let mid-session ambient mutations change behavior.

The truncation path now resolves the cap **once** at recorder creation from the captured `env`:

```ts
// inside createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder, after `env` is resolved
const eventMaxBytes = resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes(env);

// later, in buildEventLine
const boundedLine = truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent(event, line, eventMaxBytes);
```

`truncateOversizedTrajectoryEvent` no longer calls `resolveTrajectoryRuntimeEventMaxBytes()` internally; it accepts the pre-resolved `limitBytes` as a parameter. This matches the pinning semantics already used for `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY` and `resolveTrajectoryFilePath`.

### Prior review items (resolved earlier on this branch)

1. **F1 — broad SDK barrel import**: reverted to narrow subpaths.

   ```ts
   import { resolveUserPath } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime";
   import { appendRegularFile, resolveRegularFileAppendFlags } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime";
   ```

2. **F2 — re-implemented file-write safety**: restored shared helpers. The hot append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and `resolveRegularFileAppendFlags()` from `security-runtime` (no local symlink/hardlink/nlink rewrite for the append path).

3. **F3 — invalid-fallback test using a valid value**: now uses a truly malformed value.

   ```ts
   env: { OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_RUNTIME_EVENT_MAX_BYTES: "abc-not-a-size" },
   ```

4. **F4 — CHANGELOG.md conflict**: rebased on latest `main`.

### Note on `lstatSync` calls in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`

Two `lstatSync(...).isSymbolicLink()` checks remain in `writeTrajectoryPointerBestEffort`. They are **not** related to the F2 finding:

- They guard the **trajectory pointer file** (`*.trajectory-path.json`), a separate sidecar JSON file written via `openSync` + `writeFileSync` to an attacker-untrusted path.
- The trajectory main file (`*.trajectory.jsonl`) append path uses `appendRegularFile()` and is unaffected.
- These pointer-specific symlink checks were added on this PR (no equivalent in current `main`) to harden the pointer write path. They are independent of the per-event byte cap feature, but are kept because removing them would lose the symlink protection on the pointer file.

If reviewers prefer the pointer-write symlink hardening to land in a follow-up PR rather than this one, happy to move them out — please advise.

target.title: feat(gateway): support Tailscale Serve service names
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config field for Tailscale Serve.
- Passes the configured Service name through to `tailscale serve --service=<name>` while preserving the existing device-hostname behavior when unset.
- Uses a scoped Serve cleanup command for named Services so `resetOnExit` does not reset unrelated Serve config.
- Documents the new config option in the Tailscale and gateway configuration docs.

## Linked context

Closes #88629.

Related: Tailscale's current Serve CLI exposes `--service=<name>` for Services, with `--bg` and `--yes` still available on the same command.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw had no `gateway.tailscale.serviceName` config path and always configured Serve against the node hostname with `tailscale serve --bg --yes <port>`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw contributor checkout on Linux with Tailscale CLI `1.98.3` installed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `tailscale version`
  - `tailscale serve --help > /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt 2>&1`
  - `rg -n -- '--service|--bg|--yes|USAGE' /tmp/tailscale-serve-help.txt`
  - focused Vitest commands listed below
- Evidence after fix: the local Tailscale help output includes `--service value`, `--bg`, and `--yes`; the new unit coverage asserts the exact generated command arguments are `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw --bg --yes 3000` and cleanup uses `tailscale serve --service=svc:openclaw off`.
- Observed result after fix: focused tests pass and the unchanged path still calls `enableTailscaleServe(18789)` when `serviceName` is unset.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live `tailscale serve --service=...` command against a tailnet because that would mutate real Tailscale Serve/Service state from a contributor environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proves config propagation and CLI argument generation, not a live Service advertisement in a real tailnet.
- Before evidence: current `enableTailscaleServe()` accepted only `port` and emitted `['serve', '--bg', '--yes', '<port>']`; `GatewayTailscaleConfig` had only `mode`, `resetOnExit`, and `preserveFunnel`.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm test src/infra/tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts src/gateway/startup-auth.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm test src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed.
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 ...` completed on the touched TypeScript files.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, only for users who opt into `gateway.tailscale.serviceName`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes, the config schema accepts one new optional field. Existing config remains unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Yes, Tailscale Serve command construction changes when the new field is set.

What is the highest-risk area? Incorrectly resetting or overwriting unrelated Tailscale Serve state. Mitigation: the existing no-service path still uses the prior `serve reset` behavior, while the named-Service path uses a scoped `tailscale serve --service=<name> off` cleanup.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. Waiting on CI and any maintainer preference about whether this config field should also get a CLI flag in a follow-up.

commands.author: steipete

target.title: fix(auto-reply): add memory flush failure tracking with retry exhaustion
target.body:
## Summary

Fix memory flush failures permanently wedging sessions by adding per-session failure tracking, bounded retry, and a non-destructive retry-exhaustion path.

When a session exceeds the `memoryFlush` threshold, OpenClaw starts an embedded agent turn to create a handoff summary before compacting history. If that provider call fails, current `main` only logs/surfaces the error and persists no failure state. The transcript stays over threshold, so each later user message can retry the same failing flush and never reach the normal reply path.

This PR records consecutive non-abort memory flush failures and, after three failures in the same compaction cycle, marks that cycle as exhausted so the next normal reply can proceed. No transcript content is truncated or deleted by this PR.

Partially addresses #85645.

## Changes

- Add `memoryFlushFailureCount`, `memoryFlushLastFailedAt`, and `memoryFlushLastFailureError` to `SessionEntry`.
- Reserve the new session fields from plugin session-entry slots.
- On non-abort flush failure, persist failure metadata and emit `memory_flush_failed` lifecycle events.
- On successful flush, clear failure metadata.
- After 3 consecutive failures, persist `memoryFlushCompactionCount` for the current cycle, emit `memory_flush_exhausted`, and show a visible warning that memory flush is being skipped for this cycle.
- Keep abort errors retryable without recording failure metadata.
- Add regression coverage for failure tracking, abort exclusion, success cleanup, retry-before-exhaustion, and exhaustion marking.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A memoryFlush provider failure can leave the session over threshold with no durable failure state, causing repeated flush failure before normal replies can proceed.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the repository Node/Vitest harness; PR author also reported Docker `node:24-bookworm` coverage before maintainer fixup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts: 40 tests passed
pnpm check:test-types: no longer fails on agent-runner-memory.test.ts; local checkout then hit unrelated current-main doctor migration errors in src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.channels.ts
autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
git diff --check: clean
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Non-abort flush failures increment durable session failure metadata and emit lifecycle events. Before the exhaustion threshold, the next message retries memory flush. At the threshold, the current compaction cycle is marked exhausted so the normal reply path can proceed without deleting transcript history; a later compaction cycle can retry memory flush.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end provider crash with a real OpenClaw Gateway instance. The failure path is covered with `runWithModelFallback` rejection against real session-store JSON on disk.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:test-types` to confirm the PR-owned type error is gone; unrelated current-main doctor migration type errors remain locally
- [x] `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- [x] `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(ui): preserve chat sends across reconnect
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep in-flight Webchat sends tied to the current send attempt so stale pre-reconnect completions cannot remove or duplicate replayed queued messages.
- Mark stopped same-scope Gateway requests as retryable, while preventing gateway-scope changes from replaying old in-flight sends.
- Hide and ignore remove actions for messages already being sent.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (132 tests passed after rebase)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts ui/src/ui/gateway.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-queue.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-no-raw-window-open.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`

`node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` currently fails on latest `upstream/main` due to unrelated raw-copy baseline drift in `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` from upstream commit `1e3542bbe7a986ebd920a827aafe47f076f381d5`; this branch does not touch that file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Webchat queued sends survive same-gateway reconnect races (hello before stale close, ACK loss during in-flight send) without stale old `chat.send` completions removing the replayed send; gateway-scope changes do not mark old pending sends retryable.

Real environment tested: Actual local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, auth disabled on loopback port `19123`, and Google Chrome loading the real Control UI at `http://127.0.0.1:19123/chat`; plus mocked-Gateway Playwright E2E harness and focused Vitest reconnect regressions on this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
# Live Gateway + Chrome send smoke (baseline connectivity)
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/state/openclaw.json \
pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 19123 --force --verbose

node --input-type=module <local Chrome WebChat proof script>

# Reconnect/replay regressions exercised on this branch
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  -t "replays in-flight|stopped gateway client|ignores stale in-flight|retryablePending|gateway-scope|pre-ack socket close"
```

Evidence after fix:

Live Gateway + Chrome smoke (unchanged baseline):

```text
2026-05-31T10:51:35.605+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 2.4s)
2026-05-31T10:51:35.628+08:00 [gateway] ready

REAL_WEBCHAT_PROOF
url=http://127.0.0.1:19123/chat
send.method=chat.send
send.sessionKey=agent:dev:main
send.deliver=false
send.message=live proof webchat send queue smoke
send.idempotencyKey=f4aae40c-b566-4b26-a3b7-06a2ab3f1189
ack.ok=true
screenshot=/tmp/openclaw-webchat-proof.HmxgCQ/live-webchat-send.png
```

Reconnect/replay regressions (2026-05-31 local rerun on `ef8fad43e38`):

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts \
  -t "replays in-flight|stopped gateway client|ignores stale in-flight|retryablePending|gateway-scope|pre-ack socket close"

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  6 passed | 120 skipped (126)
```

Cases covered by those 6 tests:
- `replays in-flight queued chat sends when hello arrives before stale close` — fast reconnect replay with same `idempotencyKey`
- `keeps a stopped gateway client error recoverable with the same run id` — `retryablePending` stop path
- `ignores stale in-flight send completion after reconnect replay starts/succeeds` — stale completion cannot drop replayed queue item
- `retries reconnectable queued chat sends after reconnect hello` / gateway-scope gating / `pre-ack socket close` recoverable paths

Additional browser-level reconnect proof exists in `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` (`retries an ACK-lost send after reconnect with the same idempotency key`): Playwright drives the real Control UI against a mocked Gateway WebSocket, defers the first `chat.send` ACK, closes the socket, and asserts a second `chat.send` reuses the same idempotency key and clears the queue.

Observed result after fix: Same-scope reconnect replay is covered by focused unit regressions plus an existing mocked-Gateway browser E2E case; live Gateway proof confirms the real WebSocket send path still works on this branch. Stale pre-reconnect completions no longer remove replayed sends in the tested race paths.

What was not tested: A manually timed live Gateway reconnect while holding an in-flight deferred `chat.send` ACK (timing-sensitive). `checks-node-agentic-agents-core` failure on `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts:853` is unrelated to this UI-only diff.

target.title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script from stdin. Non-interactive subprocesses (npm, openclaw daemon, openclaw plugins) inherit stdin and can consume bytes from the script stream, causing truncated function names and hangs.

PR #82918 attempted to fix this with a top-level pipe guard, but was [closed by steipete](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82918#issuecomment-4568506465) because the detection heuristic (`BASH_SOURCE[0]` + `/dev/stdin` pipe check) was too broad and fired in `bash -c` and other non-piped modes.

## Fix

Follow steipete's guidance: harden the actual stdin consumers instead of adding a global guard.

- **Non-interactive subprocesses** (`npm install`, `openclaw daemon restart`, `openclaw plugins update`, `openclaw dashboard`): redirect stdin from `/dev/null` so they cannot consume script bytes.
- **Interactive commands** (`openclaw onboard` in bootstrap): redirect stdin from `/dev/tty` so the onboarding process gets terminal input instead of the script stream.
- **Wrapper functions** (`run_with_spinner`, `run_quiet_step`): add `< /dev/null` to fallback paths where raw commands run without gum's stdin protection.

Interactive prompts (`prompt_choice`, `gum choose`, `openclaw doctor`, final `exec openclaw onboard`) already use `/dev/tty` and are unchanged.

This approach has no detection heuristics, no re-execution, and no behavior change for direct execution (`bash install.sh`). Each subprocess simply gets the correct stdin for its purpose.

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: non-interactive subprocesses in install.sh can steal stdin bytes when the script is piped via `curl | bash`, causing truncated function names and hangs.
- Real environment tested: macOS, bash 3.2 and 5.2, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash)
  cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Direct dry-run (simulates bash install.sh)
  bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Verify subprocess stdin is isolated
  bash -c 'read -t 0.1 line; echo "got: ${#line} chars"' < /dev/null
  # Output: got: 0 chars
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  Stdin isolation behavior verified in the terminal:

  ```console
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line; echo "got: $line"'
  got: hello from stdin
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line < /dev/null; echo "got: $line"'
  got:
  ```

  Without `< /dev/null`, the subprocess consumes "hello from stdin" from the pipe. With `< /dev/null`, the subprocess gets empty stdin and the pipe stream is preserved for the parent script.

  Patched subprocess counts in install.sh:

  ```console
  $ grep -c '< /dev/null' scripts/install.sh
  9
  $ grep -c '< /dev/tty' scripts/install.sh
  3
  ```

  9 non-interactive subprocess calls redirect stdin from `/dev/null` (npm install, daemon restart, plugins update, dashboard, npm config, run_with_spinner fallbacks, run_quiet_step fallback). 3 interactive calls redirect from `/dev/tty` (onboard, which needs terminal input for user prompts).

  Dry-run through pipe completes without truncation:

  ```console
  $ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  $ bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: subprocesses receive `/dev/null` as stdin and cannot consume bytes from the script stream during `curl | bash` execution. Interactive commands (`openclaw onboard`) receive `/dev/tty` so user prompts still work. Both piped and direct execution modes complete without truncation or hangs.
- What was not tested: full npm install in piped mode (requires clean environment); WSL2 reproduction of the original truncation from #73814. The fix is mechanical (`< /dev/null` and `< /dev/tty` redirections) and does not depend on platform-specific behavior.

target.title: fix(update): preserve protected Codex routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve legacy `openai-codex/*` and `codex/*` protected model/runtime routes during non-interactive doctor repair instead of silently rewriting them to `openai/*`.
- Add update-time protected-route audit/rollback for core update and post-core finalization: if a protected Codex route is rewritten or deleted, restore the pre-update config and fail closed with `protected-route-drift`.
- Mark cross-provider/auth-route-changing fallbacks in fallback telemetry and keep route-changing fallback notices visible even when upstream verbose fallback output is off.
- Document the interactive-vs-non-interactive doctor behavior so operators know when routes are migrated versus preserved.
- Keep interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` migration behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

This is based on the live incident captured in #80628 and comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80628#issuecomment-4585716866.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.5.28:

- `openclaw doctor` touched `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json` at `2026-05-31T04:29:14Z`.
- A previously working Codex setup had model refs rewritten from a Codex route to OpenAI-provider refs across `agents.defaults.model.primary` and specialist-agent `model.primary` / `fallbacks` entries.
- The next isolated `News Digest` cron run failed before reply under the Codex harness with:

```text
Requested agent harness "codex" does not support openai/gpt-5.5 (provider is not one of: codex).
```

Local remediation was to patch the config back to Codex model refs, remove `openai/gpt-5.5` from the allowed agent model sets, and let the gateway hot-reload. After remediation, `openclaw models status --json` reported `defaultModel` and `resolvedDefault` as `codex/gpt-5.5` with allowed `codex/gpt-5.5` and `xai/grok-4.3`.

This PR now covers the full failure mode from #80628: doctor preservation, update audit/rollback, missing protected-route deletion detection, and visibility for cross-provider fallback transitions.

Refs #80628, #88450, #88470.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-interactive doctor repair and update/finalize flows no longer silently rewrite or delete protected Codex route model/runtime refs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; no production config or credentials.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Created a temp config containing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix`. Then created an isolated pre-update config with protected Codex primary, fallback, and model-scoped runtime routes; created a post-update config where primary/fallback were rewritten to `openai/*` and the Codex runtime route was deleted; then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart`.
- Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the patched branch:

```text
# doctor --non-interactive --fix against isolated config
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix
doctor exit=0
config after doctor:
{
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
}

preservation warning lines:
43:│  - Legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs should be rewritten to            │
49:│  - Preserved legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs during non-interactive  │

last output lines:
Updated config: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
│
└  Doctor complete.

# update/finalize against isolated before/after configs
$ HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart
exit=1
stdout:
{
  "status": "error",
  "mode": "unknown",
  "root": "/path/to/openclaw",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "steps": [],
  "durationMs": 0
}

post-core-result.json:
{
  "status": "error",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "changed": false,
  "warnings": [
    {
      "reason": "protected-route-drift",
      "message": "Protected model/runtime routes changed during update finalization; restored the pre-update config and stopped the update. agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4; agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5; agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>",
      "guidance": ["Review the config change source, then rerun openclaw update."]
    }
  ],
  "sync": {
    "changed": false,
    "errors": [
      "agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4",
      "agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5",
      "agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>"
    ]
  }
}

config after finalize:
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
        "fallbacks": ["codex/gpt-5.4"]
      },
      "models": {
        "codex/gpt-5.5": {
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.31",
    "lastTouchedAt": "<redacted timestamp>"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Doctor completed and wrote the isolated config while leaving the protected model route as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; update/finalize exited non-zero with `protected-route-drift`, wrote the post-core result file with both rewritten routes and the deleted runtime route, restored the pre-update Codex config, and did not continue plugin update/finalization.
- What was not tested: A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; proof uses isolated temp config paths only.

## Tests

Latest validation on head `ca5a65835094` after rebasing onto `origin/main` `33c246dbbaa`:

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- targeted oxlint on update/doctor proof-fix files
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies && pnpm deadcode:unused-files && pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts` — 235 tests passed
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts` — 159 tests passed
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/compaction-planning-worker.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` — 130 tests passed

target.title: fix(openai): avoid stale Responses message id replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenAI Responses request replay can reuse a signed assistant `message` item id after the corresponding `reasoning` item has been pruned from local history.
- Solution: replay signed assistant message ids only when the immediately preceding replayed item is the paired reasoning item.
- What changed: added a small replay-id policy helper, wired it into the OpenAI Responses transport, and covered the pruned-reasoning case in the existing reasoning replay tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change reasoning-item replay, tool-call replay, payload metadata, model selection, or Telegram formatting.

## Motivation

This prevents a latent OpenAI Responses failure exposed by long-running sessions whose assistant text still has a persisted Responses item id but whose paired reasoning item is no longer replayable. In that state, the next request can be rejected before the model responds.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses rejected replayed conversation history when an assistant `message` item id was included without the required paired `reasoning` item, while phase metadata still needs to survive without replaying orphan provider-owned assistant ids.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 using the OpenAI Responses transport path, plus a live OpenAI Responses API request against `gpt-5.5` on May 31, 2026 after maintainer fixup `d6902ed1a0f13c7b73e69fcfdc6566086b3c532f`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'INNER'
import OpenAI from 'openai';
import { buildOpenAIResponsesParams } from './src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts';

const model = {
  id: 'gpt-5.4',
  name: 'gpt-5.4',
  api: 'openai-responses',
  provider: 'openai',
  baseUrl: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  reasoning: true,
  input: ['text'],
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 },
  contextWindow: 128000,
  maxTokens: 4096,
};
const ZERO_USAGE = {
  input: 0,
  output: 0,
  cacheRead: 0,
  cacheWrite: 0,
  totalTokens: 0,
  cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 0 },
};
const context = {
  systemPrompt: 'system',
  messages: [
    { role: 'user', content: 'Earlier prompt', timestamp: 1 },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Earlier answer',
          textSignature: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      role: 'assistant',
      api: 'openai-responses',
      provider: 'openai',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      usage: ZERO_USAGE,
      stopReason: 'stop',
      timestamp: 2,
      content: [
        {
          type: 'thinking',
          thinking: 'chain',
          thinkingSignature: JSON.stringify({
            id: 'rs_reasoning_item',
            type: 'reasoning',
            summary: [],
          }),
        },
        {
          type: 'text',
          text: 'Pinned commentary answer',
          textSignature: JSON.stringify({ v: 1, id: 'msg_commentary', phase: 'commentary' }),
        },
      ],
    },
    { role: 'user', content: 'Latest prompt', timestamp: 3 },
  ],
};
const params = buildOpenAIResponsesParams(model, context, {});
const captured = {};
const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: 'test',
  baseURL: 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
  fetch: async (url, init) => {
    captured.url = String(url);
    captured.body = init?.body ? String(init.body) : '';
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      id: 'resp_test',
      object: 'response',
      created_at: 0,
      status: 'completed',
      model: 'gpt-5.4',
      output: [],
      parallel_tool_calls: false,
      tools: [],
    }), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
    });
  },
});
await client.responses.create({ ...params, stream: false });
const payload = JSON.stringify(params.input);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  proof: 'openai-responses-sdk-loopback',
  pr_head: 'b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60',
  request_path: new URL(captured.url).pathname,
  stale_signed_message_id: 'msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning',
  stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  stale_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_openclaw_payload: payload.includes('msg_commentary'),
  commentary_phase_id_present_in_sdk_request: captured.body.includes('msg_commentary'),
}, null, 2));
INNER
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Before this patch, the live OpenClaw Telegram agent diagnosed a real OpenAI Responses failure after conversation pruning:

```text
OpenAI rejected the next request with:
message was provided without its required reasoning item

Failure shape:
- prior assistant message ids were replayed from textSignature
- paired reasoning items had been pruned/unavailable
```

After this patch, I reran the current-head OpenAI SDK loopback on `b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60`. It imported `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, sent that exact payload through OpenAI Node SDK 6.39.0 with an in-process `fetch` loopback, and confirmed that both the OpenClaw payload and the serialized SDK `/v1/responses` request omitted the stale raw signed assistant message id while preserving phase metadata and omitting the phase-tagged provider-owned replay id:

```json
{
  "proof": "openai-responses-sdk-loopback",
  "pr_head": "b60fd8baa5901ec405764edfa476b867f65c1f60",
  "request_path": "/v1/responses",
  "stale_signed_message_id": "msg_real_response_item_requiring_reasoning",
  "stale_id_present_in_openclaw_payload": false,

After:

```
